title: Oxygastra
description: **_Oxygastra_** is a monotypic genus of dragonflies in the family Idomacromiidae. The only species, *Oxygastra curtisii*, was long considered *incertae sedis* after studies in 2007 showed it does not belong in Corduliidae. It constitutes probably the phylogenetically most isolated species in Europe and is probably its oldest relict. Based on molecular phylogenetic work it has since been placed in the family Idomacromiidae.
sources: Atlas of the European Dragonflies and Damselflies, Jean-Pierre Boudot(Editor), Vincent J Kalkman(Editor), Fons Peels(Illustrator)
Field guide to the dragonflies of Britain and Europe, Klaas-Douwe B Dijkstra.
